channel.title: Notes on Sabiqul's Braindump
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on Sabiqul's Braindump

item.title: Surgery
item.description: Textbooks Lecture notes. General surgery Notable Books Being Mortal: Illness, Medicine and What Matters in the End: Illness, Medicine and What Matters in the End

item.title: Daily Highlight
item.description: Daily highlights are tasks which bring you closer to your long term Goals and can be completed during the day (Knapp and Zeratsky 2018). It is an important technique to avoid distractions and follow through with meaningful productivity (Meurisse 2021). Trying this out on Saturday, 14 May 2022 by just making a checklist and bolding the daily highlight. Principles and Techniques The book Make Time: How to focus on what matters every day , has a whole section on techniques on how to choose a highlight and schedule it in your day.

item.title: Dopamine Detox
item.description: Related Dopamine Thoughts and Experiences My progress with a dopamine detox of sorts I also feel it ties in well with digital minimalism Categories to cut in dopamine detox Captured on: [2023-01-25 Wed 18:10], from (155) How to Stop Wasting your Life │ Full Dopamine Detox Protocol - YouTube These should be completely blocked in all ways possible Binge tech :: TV subscriptions, social media, video games, forums and political news Consumables :: alcohol, recreational drugs and junk food Thrill seeking behaviours :: gambling, shopping spree

item.title: Medicine
item.description: Areas These are smaller fields of medicine that deserve to be their own MOC , but may or may not be. It kind of depends on how many notes on that field and if I think its even worthwhile to do so. Cardiology Gastroenterology Neuroscience Psychiatry Psychology Epidemiology and Public Health Infective Diseases Respiratory Medicine Genetics Nephrology Paediatrics General Practice Obstetrics and Gynaecology General Medicine Surgery Palliative Care Medicine Notes These are links to my medicine notes (arranged alphabetically).

item.title: Maps of Content
item.description: MOCs provide a nice list of topics or nodes that might be group. Its main purpose in my view is to keep related topics within a close number of backlinks to each other. List of MOCs 4th Year Books Mathematics Medicine Neuroscience People Physics Psychology Workflows Writing

item.title: Dopamine
item.description: Dopamine constitutes about 80% of the catecholamine content in the brain. The anticipation of most rewards increases level of dopamine in the brain, and many addictive drugs increases dopamine release or block its reuptake into neurons following its release. It is synthesised from L-DOPA , which is synthesised in the brain and kidneys source Wikipedia - Dopamine Dopamine may play a role in some neural networks implicated in motivation but there are also cognitive factors and psychological factors that influence our behaviours, and these are different for each and every one of us

item.title: Medical School
item.description: Experiences at Placements Kind of wanted to do something similar to what Kay did in his book This is Going to Hurt: Secret Diaries of a Junior Doctor and write a diary of interesting events and thoughts I’ve had related to medicine. Clinical placement years seem to go by fast Couple who do not want to have medication Increased Responsibility at GP placement Leaving home early Rural Week Use of mobile phones in trauma and orthopaedics Year 2 Shadowing Day 1 Thoughts How do placements preferences work?

item.title: Hobbies
item.description: An activity done regularly done during leisure time for pleasure. While doing the activity might not be in itself pleasurable, some part of the hobby is (e.g. going to the gym might not be enjoyable but seeing improvement might be). Also see Habit . Importantly I do not think that if a hobby that produces an income (or even becomes a primary source of income) denies it from being called a hobby.

item.title: Mathematics
item.description: To Sort Chaos Theory Statistics Bayes' Theorem Regression Effect Probability Introduction to the Kelly Criterion and the “Just One More” Paradox Captured on: [2023-06-29 Thu 15:44], from (9) The “Just One More” Paradox - YouTube

item.title: Books
item.description: Categories of books These categories are meant to help you match your current energy level to the energy required to read the book. Captured on: [2023-02-09 Thu 17:09], from You Don’t Hate Reading: How to Easily Read 100 Books This Year - YouTube . I have not decided to categorise the books I read into these categories because I don’t really have a need to. At the moment I’m still trying to build the habit of reading, so just forcing myself to read when and where I can.

item.title: Bayes' Theorem
Habit
item.description: Habit Building 2 Day Rule Captured on: [2022-05-05 Thu 23:16], from How to Break Bad Habits & Build Good Ones - YouTube Kharma Medic Not allowed to skip 2 days when it comes to building a habit. 2 day rule can be used for bad habits as well Captured on: [2023-03-05 Sun 13:15], from The 2-year vs 2-day vs 2-min Rule - YouTube Make habits that will work during the most busy times Captured on: [2022-06-11 Sat 04:30], from Parkinson’s Law (and how to finish work with time to spare) - YouTube When making habits, make sure that they are able to be followed even during the most busy of times.

item.title: Butterfly Effect
item.description: In chaos theory , the butterfly effect is the sensitive dependence on initial conditions in which a small change in one state of a deterministic nonlinear system can result in large differences in a later state. The term is closely associated with the work of mathematician and meteorologist Edward Lorenz. Reflections and Experiences Research I suppose this is more of the notion of having to start somewhere, but one instance of the butterfly effect for me was when I decided to become the IT officer for the medical school association.

item.title: Chaos Theory
item.description: Definition Theory and branch of mathematics focused on the study of chaos which are dynamical systems whose apparently random states of disorder and irregularities are actually governed by underlying patterns and deterministic laws that are highly sensitive to initial conditions

item.title: Clinical placement years seem to go by fast
item.description: Just started placements (from [2023-01-31 Tue]) but cannot remember anything special. I realised this when reading Make Time: How to focus on what matters every day . Unfortunately this seems to be the case with many parts of life and trying to appreciate the every day parts of life seems to be harder and harder without getting into something like Journalling . Regardless with how future-orientated medicine is in addition to how repetitive the clinical years of placement can be, it can be easy to feel like the year went by without many notable things happening.

item.title: Dopamine Detox: A Short Guide to Remove Distractions and Get Your Brain to Do Hard Things
item.description: author Meurisse, t. tags Dopamine Detox keywords nil related nil recommended by Kindle recommendations, free from kindle unlimited finished [2022-10-16 Sun] (a couple of days before this) DONE Kindle Highlights Activities Decide tasks you can and can’t do during your dopamine detox Remove notifications and distractions Also remove phone Take notes of your feelings during the dopamine detox Do not check phone on waking up I’ve noticed that if I check my phone or access the internet upon waking up, I will probably become distracted.

item.title: Regression Effect
item.description: Captured on: [2022-04-23 Sat 00:25] from https://www.asc.ohio-state.edu/calder.13/stat528/Lectures/lecture9_2slides.PDF Tags Statistics Regression Effect: In virtually all test-retest situations, the bottom group on the first test will on average show some improvement on the second test and the top group will on average fall back. This effect is known as the regression effect. Example Captured on: [2022-04-23 Sat 00:40], from Regression toward the mean - Wikipedia Consider a class of students taking a 100-item true/false test on a subject.

item.title: Writing
item.description: I would like to call writing a hobby of mine but frankly I don’t do it enough. I have a blog of sorts. See also Writing Workflow Academic Writing Essay Writing Journalling

item.title: Yak Shaving
item.description: Captured on: [2022-12-10 Sat 17:01], from Yak Shaving You see, yak shaving is what you are doing when you’re doing some stupid, fiddly little task that bears no obvious relationship to what you’re supposed to be working on, but yet a chain of twelve causal relations links what you’re doing to the original meta-task. Often find this to be the case when using Emacs . Like I could try to capture a new task and then realise that this can be improved by changing my configuration and it begins a rabit hole.
